rules.statement: Every autonomous proposal commit's actual diff (git diff --name-only pre_execution_sha post_execution_sha) MUST be a subset of its declared production set (the union of _sandbox_target_paths observed by SandboxLifecycle and files_produced declared by the action). A commit that includes files outside this set contains bytes the action did not author and violates ADR-101 D1. Checked post-commit by CommitAuthorshipAuditWorker against core.proposal_consequences. Pre-ADR-129 entries with empty declared_production are skipped as unverifiable.
rules.rationale: ADR-101 D1 establishes that a commit's diff must contain only bytes its author produced. ADR-129 D1/D2 close the write-side race (commit_paths staging check) and persist the production claim in core.proposal_consequences. This rule operationalises D1 as a post-commit audit invariant. Reporting rather than blocking because post-commit detection is the current implementation constraint; promotion to blocking requires a pre-push hook (ADR-129 D6 follow-on). Grounding: ADR-129 D3; detection: CommitAuthorshipAuditWorker (.intent/workers/commit_authorship_audit_worker.yaml); remediation: PENDING in auto_remediation.yaml (no autonomous fix; requires governor investigation).
